steps.title: Compare the data values
steps.content: The example must show that elevation and prominence can differ significantly. A mountain with a high elevation but much lower prominence directly illustrates why elevation alone may not indicate how high the mountain appears.

steps.title: Reject incomplete comparisons
steps.content: Choice B compares only prominence values. Choice C adds mountain-range information that does not show elevation versus prominence. Choice D gives elevation and range but not prominence, so it cannot demonstrate the difference. The answer is A.
